damage.value: life-drain d10 (hand, close, area, ignores armor)

system.concept: A black-iron-cursed fae husk that devours all life around it.
system.qualities: Senses life; surrounded by inert death and ash. Armor 5 (resilience, lacks vitals), 1 vs. black iron — and can be truly destroyed only by black iron.
system.description: A Fae wounded by black iron will pray for death, for to survive such a wound is a curse. They grow weak, then confused, then hungry, until they become a walking husk, surrounded by death, inhaling any source of life they find. Hollow Fae are no longer vulnerable to mere iron; debts and promises no longer bind them; they will tear their way free of any domain. Only black iron can destroy them — and what living Fae would be fool enough to try? A large Hollow Fae is tougher (23 HP, +1 damage, +reach); a huge one tougher still (27 HP, +3 damage, +reach).

items.name: Pause in confusion when confronted with who it once was
Open wide, drinking in the vitality of the living (inflicting debilities)
